findings.title: mini_summary renders twice when lesson has find_bug but no connection_to_game
findings.description: When lesson.find_bug is truthy, lesson.connection_to_game is falsy, and lesson.mini_summary is truthy, the mini_summary renders both inside the 2-column grid (lines 279-283 as the else branch) AND again outside the grid (lines 295-299). This duplicates content visually.
findings.fix: Remove the outer conditional block (lines 295-299) — the inner block already covers this case.

findings.title: crypto.randomUUID() has no fallback
findings.description: If crypto.randomUUID() is unavailable (non-HTTPS context, older browsers), getUserId() throws. Since every authenticated API call depends on this, the entire app breaks silently. authHeaders() has no try/catch, and callers like useProgress() catch generically — so a user in an insecure context sees zero progress with no actionable feedback.
findings.fix: Add a fallback: `uid = crypto.randomUUID?.() ?? Date.now().toString(36) + Math.random().toString(36).slice(2)`

findings.title: SteampunkCard component redefined on every render
findings.description: The SteampunkCard component is defined inside the LessonPage function body. Every state change causes React to see a new component type, forcing unmount/remount of all children wrapped in it. This destroys internal state (e.g. expanded states in children) and harms performance.
findings.fix: Move SteampunkCard outside the component (top-level or a separate file).

findings.title: CourseMap uses <a href> instead of React Router <Link>
findings.description: Lesson links use `<a href="/lesson/${lesson.id}">` instead of React Router's `<Link to=...>`. This causes a full page reload on every lesson click, resetting all React state (Pyodide, quiz state, editor content). This is a significant UX degradation.
findings.fix: Replace `<a>` with `<Link to={...}>` from react-router-dom.

findings.title: Reviews fetch has no loading state and silent error catch
findings.description: The /api/reviews fetch in HomePage has `.catch(() => {})` which silently swallows all errors. There is no loading spinner or error message. If the API is slow or unreachable, the reviews section appears empty with no visual feedback. Also, no AbortController cleanup — if the component unmounts before the fetch completes, it calls setState on an unmounted component.
findings.fix: Add loading/error states to the reviews fetch; add AbortController cleanup.

findings.title: useLessons() error not surfaced in Layout or Sidebar
findings.description: useLessons() exposes an `error` state, but Layout.tsx (which calls it) never passes it to the Sidebar or displays it. If the /api/lessons endpoint fails, the app renders with an empty lessons array and no feedback to the user — the sidebar is empty, navigation links vanish.
findings.fix: Pass the error to a toast/notification or show inline in the Sidebar.

findings.title: Line numbers have insufficient color contrast (~3.47:1)
findings.description: Line numbers use color #6b7280 on background #0d0c14. The contrast ratio is approximately 3.47:1, which fails WCAG AA (4.5:1 for normal text). This affects readability, especially since these are 10px text.
findings.fix: Lighten line numbers to at least #8b8f98 or darken the background.

findings.title: Code textarea lacks accessible label
findings.description: The main code textarea in CodePlayground has no aria-label, aria-labelledby, or associated <label> element. Screen readers will not announce its purpose.
findings.fix: Add `aria-label="Python code editor"` or `aria-labelledby` pointing to a visible heading.

findings.title: Loading spinner missing aria-hidden and aria-label
findings.description: The animated loading spinner uses `progress_activity` without `aria-hidden="true"` or `role="status"` / `aria-live="polite"`. Screen readers may read the icon character or ignore the loading state entirely.
findings.fix: Add `aria-hidden="true"` on the icon and a visually-hidden `role="status"` span with 'Loading lesson...'.

findings.title: useProgress() has no loading state exposed
findings.description: useProgress() starts with an empty progress map and fetches asynchronously. There is no `loading` boolean returned. Components using it (HomePage, LessonPage, CourseMap) show '0 completed' / 'locked' for all lessons until the API responds, causing a flash of incorrect state.
findings.fix: Add a `loading` boolean to the return value and show a skeleton while loading.

findings.title: Pyodide load failure has no retry mechanism
findings.description: If Pyodide fails to load (script.onerror), status is set to 'idle' and the user sees 'Python ne zagruzilsya. Prover internet.' when trying to run code. There is no retry button — the user must refresh the page.
findings.fix: Add a 'Retry' button that resets scriptLoaded and re-creates the <script> tag.

findings.title: Duplicate BASE constant defined in HomePage
findings.description: HomePage.tsx defines `const BASE = '/api'` at line 8, but useApi.ts already defines `const BASE = '/api'` at line 5. The HomePage fetch at line 30 uses `${BASE}/reviews` instead of the shared hook useReviews() defined in useApi.ts.
findings.fix: Use the existing useReviews() hook instead of duplicating the fetch and constant.

findings.title: QuizSection catches errors but shows no user-facing feedback
findings.description: When checkQuizAnswer() throws, the catch block sets a fallback result but only logs to console. The UI shows 'Неверно' with an empty correct_id — preventing the user from learning the right answer. If the API is unreachable, this is misleading.
findings.fix: Show a distinct 'Connection error' message when the fetch fails, preserving the correct answer display.

findings.title: Bagus error feedback uses animate-bounce, which can trigger vestibular issues
findings.description: The error feedback box uses `animate-bounce` — a CSS animation that repeatedly bounces the element. This animation cannot be disabled by users who prefer reduced motion (no `prefers-reduced-motion` check).
findings.fix: Use `motion-safe:animate-bounce` or `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference)` guard.

findings.title: checkQuizAnswer and checkWhatOutputs have no request timeout
findings.description: Both functions make fetch() calls without a timeout or AbortSignal. A slow/unresponsive API will cause the UI to hang indefinitely (QuizSection's button remains disabled, MissionCard shows 'checking').
findings.fix: Pass an AbortSignal with a timeout (e.g., 10s) to fetch.

findings.title: Unused import: DialogueBubble
findings.description: DialogueBubble is imported at line 6 but never used in the component. DialogueScene is used instead, which may internally use DialogueBubble. This can cause lint warnings and confusion.
findings.fix: Remove the unused DialogueBubble import.

findings.title: Conditional 2-column grid wraps single-child sections
findings.description: The grid at line 247 wraps PredictOutputBlock and GameRelevanceBlock. If either is missing, the grid still renders with a single cell. This can look unbalanced. Same for the FindBugBlock grid at line 270.
findings.fix: Conditionally render the grid wrapper only when both children are present, or use auto-fill.

findings.title: Continue Quest button may become invisible if all lessons completed
findings.description: The 'Continue Quest' button finds the first incomplete lesson. If all lessons are complete, `next` is undefined and `navigate(undefined)` does nothing silently — the button still renders but is dead.
findings.fix: Show 'All quests complete!' or navigate to a review/celebration page when done.

findings.title: CourseMap redundant totalLessons/completedCount from useProgress vs props
findings.description: CourseMap receives `lessons` as a prop AND calls useProgress() for progress data. But it also destructures `totalLessons` and `completedCount` from useProgress() while computing `total` from both. If the two progress sources disagree, the UI is inconsistent.
findings.fix: Use a single source of truth — either props or the hook, not both.

findings.title: isLessonUnlocked return true when lessons array is empty (loading)
findings.description: When lessons array is empty (still loading), isLessonUnlocked returns true because findIndex returns -1 and idx <= 0 is true. This means locked lessons shown briefly as unlocked before the correct state loads from the API.
findings.fix: Add a loading guard — if lessons array is empty, show a skeleton instead of rendering lesson content.

top_3_fixes: 1. Move SteampunkCard outside LessonPage to prevent unmount/remount on every render (critical performance bug).
2. Fix mini_summary duplication in LessonPage lines 295-299 (critical logic bug).
3. Replace <a href> with React Router <Link> in CourseMap to prevent full page reloads (critical navigation bug).
